payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because Brain prefers the latest active durable memory for the same subject/context. mem_old was excluded because its status indicates it has been superseded by mem_new, and superseded memories are filtered from current recall to avoid presenting outdated facts as current.
payload.decision: Recall selected mem_new as the current durable memory for Sam at Goldman because mem_old is marked superseded and therefore filtered from current-state results.

entity_resolution.reason: The query explicitly targets Sam in the Goldman context, and recall explains selection among existing memories rather than creating or changing identity resolution.

memory_cards.statement: Sam is associated with Goldman according to mem_new, which is the active memory returned by recall.
mem_old was filtered because it is superseded, so it is not presented as the current fact.

details: Identified mem_new as the active record returned by recall.
Identified mem_old as filtered due to superseded status.
Explained behavior using current-state recall rules that exclude outdated memories.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded_by link to mem_new.
Check whether both memories are scoped to the same entity pair: Sam and Goldman.
If mem_old should still be visible, query history/audit mode instead of current-state recall.
If the supersession was applied incorrectly, remove or correct the superseded status/link.
